source_jobs.title: Associate Theft Investigator
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/d79356cb-21b1-41b5-8e25-ceef94f4456f/a61ce539-840a-4d27-90ad-388bb2acb3f6/For%20Ashby%20posting.png]




ABOUT PANOPTYC

At Panoptyc, we're on a mission to revolutionize loss prevention. Using visual AI and manual reviewers, we help retailers detect theft in over 15,000 markets across the United States. From Fortune 500 giants to local businesses, our impact is far-reaching. As a fully remote, rapidly growing team, we're excited to invite top talent worldwide to join us in reshaping the future of retail security.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're seeking detail-oriented Associate Theft Investigators to analyze video footage from self-service stores and identify potential theft incidents. This role combines analytical skills with attention to detail to support our mission of reducing retail losses.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Conduct thorough analysis of surveillance footage to identify and document theft incidents

 - Classify and interpret events in video feeds according to established guidelines

 - Process theft reports and maintain accurate documentation

 - Collaborate with team members through internal communication channels

 - Provide customer support and manage operational tasks as needed

 - Update and maintain theft tracking systems




REQUIREMENTS

 - Strong attention to detail and analytical skills

 - Ability to maintain focus while reviewing video content

 - Excellent written communication skills




DEVICE AND INTERNET REQUIREMENTS

 - Windows:
   
   - OS: Windows 10 or 11
   
   - Processor: Intel i5, i7, or higher / AMD Ryzen 5, 7, or higher
   
   - RAM: 16gb or higher
   
   - Storage/Memory: 256gb or higher

 - Apple Mac
   
   - OS: Ventura (version 13 or higher) / Sonoma (version 14 or higher) / Sequoia (version 15 or higher) / Tahoe (version 26 or higher)
   
   - Processor: Intel i5 or i7 / Apple M1 chip or higher
   
   - RAM: 16gb or higher
   
   - Storage: 256gb or higher

 - Internet Connection
   
   - 50mbps or higher




MUST BE ABLE TO

 - Attend the 2-day self-paced training program

 - Successfully complete the 2-week nesting period




WHY JOIN US?

 - Reach earnings of up to $80 USD per week!

 - Flexible working hours after upon endorsement to operations

 - Full remote setup (WFH)

source_jobs.title: Billing and Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/d79356cb-21b1-41b5-8e25-ceef94f4456f/c6b22cd4-7b2c-4cc3-b8df-0f5dbd765621/Screenshot%202025-04-03%20at%209.42.43%E2%80%AFAM.png]

About Panoptyc

At Panoptyc, we're on a mission to revolutionize loss prevention. Using visual AI and manual reviewers, we help retailers detect theft in over 15,000 markets across the United States. From Fortune 500 giants to local businesses, our impact is far-reaching. As a fully remote, rapidly growing team, we're excited to invite top talent worldwide to join us in reshaping the future of retail security.



About the Role
We're seeking an experienced Billing and Operations Specialist to ensure accurate financial processing and operational support during our high-growth phase. In this crucial role, you'll oversee invoicing, purchase orders, payroll tracking, and monthly financial processes. You'll have the opportunity to streamline operational workflows, maintain performance tracking systems, and support finance-related tasks across teams.



Responsibilities:

 - Ensure accurate and timely financial processing to support the company’s growth and operational efficiency.

 - Streamline operational workflows while maintaining performance tracking systems across teams.

 - Process invoices and subscriptions, and maintain accurate daily financial tracking reports

 - Provide support in verifying invoices, confirming the accuracy, completeness, and validity of billing details

 - Create, manage, and track purchase orders.

 - Handle subscription billing processes, including account acquisitions and transfers.

 - Provide support for account acquisition processes and renewal management.

 - Assist with the preparation of credit memos, estimates, and customer quotes as needed.

 - Update and monitor weekly performance metrics and reports.

 - Support month-end closing activities, including recurring and ad hoc invoicing, as well as system and location updates.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Invoicing and Fulfillment teams to ensure accurate and efficient billing operations.

 - Handle ad hoc operational and financial tasks in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
   

Requirements:

 - Proven experience in billing, finance operations, or a related administrative role.

 - Familiarity with invoicing systems, purchase order processes, and financial reconciliation.

 - Highly detail-oriented with excellent organizational skills.

 - Strong problem-solving abilities and a proactive mindset in addressing operational issues.

 - Strong communication skills and ability to work effectively in a remote environment.

 - Availability to work during US business hours.
   
   

Bonus Points:

 - Experience with QBO, inventory management or e-commerce operational tools.

 - Background in retail, logistics, or fulfillment-based operations.

 - Previous work in a high-growth startup environment.
   

Why Join Panoptyc?

 - Competitive compensation based on experience.

 - Be part of a rapidly growing team reshaping retail security.

 - Full remote work flexibility with a dynamic, global team.
   

Job Details:

 - Full-time status (40 hours per week)

 - Able to work during US Eastern time zone 

 - Salary: $6 USD/hr

 - Location: Philippines
   




Let’s protect retail together! 🚀

source_jobs.title: Lead Embedded Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/d79356cb-21b1-41b5-8e25-ceef94f4456f/b19305ba-9bc6-44d5-9c47-21666f7e5a52/1751318206949.jpeg]




Lead Embedded Systems Engineer

Location: Remote
Department: Engineering · Edge Infrastructure
Reports To: CTO
Type: Full-Time

About Panoptyc

Panoptyc is an AI-powered retail security and loss prevention platform purpose-built for the micromarket, convenience store, and enterprise retail segments. Our computer vision stack runs at the edge — directly on devices deployed in client environments — to deliver real-time shrink detection, transaction verification, and operational intelligence at scale. We serve enterprise customers and we're growing fast.

This is a high-leverage role at the core of our physical product. The work you do here ships to real hardware in real stores, and the quality of it directly determines the reliability of the platform our customers depend on.

The Role

We're looking for a Lead Embedded Systems Engineer who knows Linux inside and out, loves solving hard problems in the real world, and has a track record of building and shipping things — not just as part of a big team, but on their own. If you have side projects, open source contributions, or personal builds that you can talk about in detail, we want to hear about them.

You'll own the edge device platform end to end — from deployment pipelines and runtime orchestration to remote management and camera integrations in the field. You'll work closely with our ML, backend, and product teams to ensure inference workloads, camera feeds, and device fleets all run reliably in uncontrolled retail environments.

This is not a role for someone who prefers clean lab conditions. It's for someone who gets energized by the complexity of the real world — and who will use every tool available, including LLMs, to move fast and figure things out.

What You'll Own

Edge Device Platform

 - Deploy, configure, and maintain edge compute solutions on NVIDIA Jetson and similar embedded Linux platforms

 - Own hardware validation for new deployments, balancing compute headroom, thermal constraints, cost, and supply chain reliability

 - Architect and maintain systemd service definitions for reliable, observable, auto-recovering edge processes

 - Build and manage Docker container orchestration strategies for running CV inference workloads at the edge

Cloud Connectivity & Remote Management

 - Own our AWS IoT Core integration — device provisioning, shadow state, telemetry pipelines, and fleet-wide configuration

 - Build robust OTA update and rollback mechanisms that account for unreliable field connectivity

 - Design and maintain remote observability and recovery automation across distributed device fleets

Camera & Retail Systems Integration

 - Integrate with IP camera ecosystems and retail systems in the field

 - Ensure video pipeline reliability including reconnection logic, frame integrity checks, and latency-aware buffering

AI Workload Optimization

 - Work with our computer vision team to ensure inference workloads run reliably on constrained edge hardware

 - Profile and optimize memory, thermal, and power envelopes on edge hardware with acceptable duty cycles

Engineering Culture & Tooling

 - Actively leverage AI coding tools and LLM-assisted workflows as a force multiplier — this is an expectation, not a differentiator

 - Document architecture, deployment runbooks, and failure modes rigorously

 - Collaborate across engineering, product, and installation/support teams

What We're Looking For

Required

 - 5+ years of hands-on experience with embedded Linux systems in production environments

 - Comfort with Linux system administration — you know your way around a Linux system deeply, whether that's on a device or a server

 - Familiarity with the Yocto Project — you don't need to be an expert, but you can't be someone who looks at it and says "this is too complicated"

 - Solid Docker experience including multi-s

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative (BDR)
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/d79356cb-21b1-41b5-8e25-ceef94f4456f/736694b3-6117-4ee6-a4fe-d24f58adcc27/Screenshot%202026-04-16%20at%2014.51.32.png]


BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT REPRESENTATIVE (BDR)

Location: Remote - LatAm preferred, Eastern Europe/exceptional candidates elsewhere

Team: Sales




ABOUT PANOPTYC

At Panoptyc, we're on a mission to revolutionize loss prevention. Using visual AI and manual reviewers, we help retailers detect theft in over 15,000 markets across the United States. From Fortune 500 giants to local businesses, our impact is far-reaching. As a fully remote, rapidly growing team, we're excited to invite top talent to join us in reshaping the future of retail security.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring a Business Development Representative (BDR) who is a relentless hunter and a master of first impressions. This is not a passive "inbound" role; you will be the engine of our outbound growth, targeting key decision-makers in the retail and grocery sectors.

You will own the top of the sales funnel and are responsible for:

 - High-volume outbound prospecting (Cold calling, emailing, social selling)

 - Qualifying leads and setting high-quality meetings for Account Executives

 - Building a consistent pipeline of enterprise and mid-market opportunities

 - Mastering our value proposition to navigate gatekeepers and reach executives

You must be hungry, resilient, and systematically organized. We value grit and the ability to iterate quickly based on market feedback.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

Pipeline Generation

 - Execute a high-velocity outreach strategy to generate qualified discovery calls

 - Identify and research target accounts within the retail, grocery, and multi-location sectors

 - Personalize outreach at scale while maintaining a high volume of daily activities

Strategic Prospecting

 - Map out complex enterprise organizations to identify key stakeholders (Directors of LP, VPs of Operations, C-Level)

 - Utilize a multi-channel approach (phone, email, LinkedIn, and video) to break into high-value accounts

 - Experiment with messaging and playbooks to improve conversion rates

Qualification & Handoff

 - Conduct initial discovery calls to qualify prospects based on pain points, budget, and authority

 - Seamlessly transition qualified leads to Account Executives with detailed notes and context

 - Ensure every prospect interaction is logged accurately in the CRM

Market Intelligence

 - Gather insights from conversations to help refine our ICP (Ideal Customer Profile)

 - Act as the "eyes and ears" of the company, reporting on competitor moves and market trends

 - Provide feedback to Marketing on lead quality and messaging resonance

What “Great” Looks Like in This Role

We hire great. In this role, that means:

 - You treat every "No" as a step closer to a "Yes"

 - You answer questions directly and handle objections with poise

 - You are systematic about your calendar and your CRM hygiene

 - You don't just follow a script; you understand the why behind the pain points

 - You demonstrate urgency, ownership, and a competitive drive to hit your numbers


REQUIREMENTS

Experience

 - 2–4 years of experience in Sales Development, Business Development, Lead Generation, or Outbound Sales (SaaS experience preferred)

 - Proven track record of meeting or exceeding activity metrics and qualified meeting quotas

 - Experience navigating complex organizations to reach decision-makers

 - Traveling: This role might envolve quarterly travel (3 times per year) for U.S.-based conferences and on-site client engagements.

Sales Muscle

 - Comfortable making 60+ cold calls a day

 - Strong HubSpot hygiene and appointment setting

 - Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, your emails are concise and action-oriented

 - Ability to handle rejection professionally and stay motivated

Analytical & Systematic

 - Highly organized approach to territory management and lead follow-up

 - Fam

notes: Panoptyc is a retail security and loss prevention platform using visual AI and manual reviewers. The company is fully remote and rapidly growing.
